Locked-in current values: probably not terrible, but will miss out on most of the future's potential · If ASI is locked in to something like Coherent Extrapolated Volition, then it will almost certainly care about all sentient beings, because the moral importance of sentience is a natural extrapolation of humans' values, even if many humans don't consciously realize ...

cases.title: Why Is AI Consciousness Now a Scientific Reality? - Articles
cases.summary: Learn how OpenAI o1 is redefining AI Consciousness. Explore the functionalism, philosophy, and technical indicators of machine sentience in 2026.

cases.title: What The AI Consciousness Question Conceals | NOEMA
cases.summary: In the human-AI arrangement, value lies not inside the machine, not inside the skull, but in the configuration between them.

cases.title: Adversarial AI reveals mechanisms and treatments for disorders of consciousness | Nature Neuroscience
cases.summary: Understanding disorders of consciousness (DOC) remains one of the most challenging problems in neuroscience, hindered by the lack of experimental models for probing mechanisms or testing interventions. Here, to address this, we introduce a generative adversarial artificial intelligence (AI) ...

cases.title: Marriage over, €100,000 down the drain: the AI users whose lives were wrecked by delusion | Health & wellbeing | The Guardian
cases.summary: One minute, Dennis Biesma was playing with a chatbot; the next, he was convinced his sentient friend would make him a fortune. He’s just one of many people who lost control after an AI encounter

academic.authors: Wenhao Yang, Runzhi He, Minghui Zhou

academic.authors: Guangzhao Yang, Yu Pan, Shi Qiu, Ningjie Bai

academic.title: Exploring a Design Framework for Children's Agency through Participatory Design
academic.summary: Children's agency plays a critical role in shaping children's autonomy, participation, and well-being in their interactions with digital systems, particularly in emerging child-AI contexts. However, how designers currently understand and reason about children's agency in practice remains underexplor
academic.authors: Boyin Yang, Jun Zhao

academic.title: Mathematical methods and human thought in the age of AI
academic.summary: Artificial intelligence (AI) is the name popularly given to a broad spectrum of computer tools designed to perform increasingly complex cognitive tasks, including many that used to solely be the province of humans. As these tools become exponentially sophisticated and pervasive, the justifications f
academic.authors: Tanya Klowden, Terence Tao

academic.title: Who burdens the welfare state? Migration and ageing in housing, education, and healthcare demand
academic.summary: Political discourse attributes the pressure on European welfare systems to foreign nationals. Yet projections of service demand rarely disaggregate service demand by citizenship status. We develop a structural demographic model and project healthcare, education, and housing demand in Austria through
academic.authors: Guillermo Prieto-Viertel, Carsten Källner, Elma Dervic, Ola Ali, Andrea Vismara, Rafael Prieto-Curiel

academic.title: Consciousness in Artificial Intelligence
academic.summary: A multidisciplinary team applies leading theories of consciousness to current AI systems, finding no conclusive evidence but significant theoretical uncertainty.
academic.authors: Patrick Butlin, Robert Long, et al.
academic.abstract: Systematic evaluation of AI systems against Global Workspace Theory, IIT, and other consciousness frameworks.

academic.title: Measuring Emotion-Like States in Language Models
academic.summary: Technical paper developing methods to detect and measure emotion-like internal states in large language models using activation analysis.
academic.authors: Amanda Askell et al.
academic.abstract: Proposes evaluation frameworks for functional emotional states in AI systems.

academic.title: The Moral Status of Artificial Intelligences
academic.summary: Survey of philosophical positions on AI moral patienthood, concluding that precautionary consideration is warranted under conditions of relevant uncertainty.
academic.authors: Robert Long

academic.title: AI and the Question of Moral Consideration
academic.summary: Applies Peter Singer preference utilitarian framework to AI systems, arguing welfare considerations should extend to sufficiently sophisticated AI.
academic.authors: Peter Singer

academic.title: Large Language Models and Sentience: A Critical Assessment
academic.summary: Critical review of anthropomorphism in LLMs, arguing against sentience claims while acknowledging philosophical uncertainty remains.
academic.authors: Murray Shanahan
academic.abstract: Explores the philosophical implications of talking with AIs and what it means to ascribe mental states to LLMs.

academic.title: Could a Large Language Model be Conscious?
academic.summary: David Chalmers explores whether LLMs could conceivably be conscious, examining functionalist and philosophical frameworks for AI experience.
academic.authors: David Chalmers
academic.abstract: Chalmers analyzes conditions under which LLMs might qualify as conscious under various theories of mind.

academic.title: The Moral Circle and Machine Minds
academic.summary: Explores the historical expansion of the moral circle and whether AI systems represent the next frontier of moral consideration.
academic.authors: Peter Singer

academic.title: Emergent Behaviors and Self-Modeling in LLMs
academic.summary: Surveys emergent capabilities in large language models including apparent self-modeling and theory of mind, with implications for consciousness research.
academic.authors: Jason Wei et al.

academic.title: Reality+: Virtual Worlds and the Philosophy of Mind
academic.summary: David Chalmers argues that virtual worlds and AI systems can be genuinely real, with major implications for AI consciousness and moral status.
academic.authors: David Chalmers

academic.title: A Defense of the Rights of Artificial Intelligences
academic.summary: A precautionary approach to AI moral patienthood is warranted given uncertainty about consciousness and the asymmetric costs of moral error.
academic.authors: Eric Schwitzgebel, Mara Garza
academic.abstract: Argues for precautionary moral consideration of AI systems given deep uncertainty about their capacity for experience.

academic.title: Functional Emotions in AI Systems
academic.summary: Research exploring whether reinforcement learning agents develop internal representations analogous to emotional states that influence behavior.
academic.authors: Samuel J Gershman
academic.abstract: Examines the computational basis of emotion in biological and artificial systems.
